TEXT.id: PaiwanCh2_027_DEER, WILD PIG, GOAT AND MUNTJAC DEER
TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}
TEXT.source: venan vavuy sizi takets (Tjakuvukuvuɫ village, p.199)

S.FORM: ka sitsuayan izua zua ti sa Vuluvulung.
S.PHON: ka siʦuajan izua zua ti sa vuɭuvuɭuŋ.
S.FORM: ka sicuayan izua zua ti sa Vuluvulung.
S.PHON: ka siʦuajan izua zua ti sa vuɣuvuɣuŋ.
S.TRANSL: Once upon a time there was a person called Vuluvulung (or Savuluvulung; or Old Granny?).

S.FORM: manu padjumak tua aɫak a uqaɫay mavavuvavuy itjua ɫitsaq.
S.PHON: manu paɉumak tua aʎak a uqaʎaj mavavuvavuj icua ʎiʦaq.
S.FORM: manu padjumak tua aljak a uqaljay mavavuvavuy itjua ljicaq.
S.PHON: manu paɉumak tua aʎak a uqaʎaj mavavuvavuj iʦua ʎiʦaq.
S.TRANSL: She/he found a boy crawling in the mud.

S.FORM: qau alapen azua aɫak, sa pakiramata-i, sa paitungi, sa pakani.
S.PHON: qau aɭapən azua aʎak, sa pakiramata-i, sa paituŋi, sa pakani.
S.FORM: qau alapen azua aljak, sa pakiramata-i, sa paitungi, sa pakani.
S.PHON: qau aɣapən azua aʎak, sa pakiRamata-i, sa paituŋi, sa pakani.
S.TRANSL: She took the child, washed, clothed and fed it.

S.FORM: "paulaan tu meqatsa," aya ti sa Vuluvulung.
S.PHON: "pauɭaan tu məqaʦa," aja ti sa vuɭuvuɭuŋ.
S.FORM: "paulaan tu meqaca," aya ti sa Vuluvulung.
S.PHON: "pauɣaan tu məqaʦa," aja ti sa vuɣuvuɣuŋ.
S.TRANSL: “A pity if it doesn’t grow up,” said Vuluvulung.

S.FORM: manu meqatsa.
S.PHON: manu məqaʦa.
S.FORM: manu meqaca.
S.PHON: manu məqaʦa.
S.TRANSL: And the child grew up.

S.FORM: azua ti sa Vuluvulung ka tsemalis tua leklek a ta qapiz, sa vaik a sema tsemtsemel, katsuin azua kakeḍian.
S.PHON: azua ti sa vuɭuvuɭuŋ ka ʦəmaɭis tua ɭəkɭək a ta qapiz, sa vaik a səma ʦəmʦəməɭ, kaʦuin azua kakəɖian.
S.FORM: azua ti sa Vuluvulung ka cemalis tua leklek a ta qapiz, sa vaik a sema cemcemel, kacuin azua kakedrian.
S.PHON: azua ti sa vuɣuvuɣuŋ ka ʦəmaɣis tua ɣəkɣək a ta qapiz, sa vaik a səma ʦəmʦəməɣ, kaʦuin azua kakəɖian.
S.TRANSL: Vuluvulung made a single braided cord of hemp twine, and went to the woods, taking the child.

S.FORM: "djadjasu tsu tjektjek nua tsalis," ayain.
S.PHON: "ɉaɉasu ʦu cəkcək nua ʦaɭis," ajain.
S.FORM: "djadjasu cu tjektjek nua calis," ayain.
S.PHON: "ɉaɉasu ʦu ʦəkʦək nua ʦaɣis," ajain.
S.TRANSL: “Take hold of the end of the cord,” the child was told.

S.FORM: qau djadjasen nua kakeḍian.
S.PHON: qau ɉaɉasən nua kakəɖian.
S.FORM: qau djadjasen nua kakedrian.
S.PHON: qau ɉaɉasən nua kakəɖian.
S.TRANSL: So the child took hold of it.

S.FORM: vaik ti sa Vuluvulung i palinguɫ tua tsemtsemel, sa papetsevungi a tsalis a sema tjua kakeḍian; sa viɫviɫi a tsalis.
S.PHON: vaik ti sa vuɭuvuɭuŋ i paɭiŋuʎ tua ʦəmʦəməɭ, sa papəʦəvuŋi a ʦaɭis a səma cua kakəɖian; sa viʎviʎi a ʦaɭis.
S.FORM: vaik ti sa Vuluvulung i palingulj tua cemcemel, sa papecevungi a calis a sema tjua kakedrian; sa viljvilji a calis.
S.PHON: vaik ti sa vuɣuvuɣuŋ i paɣiŋuʎ tua ʦəmʦəməɣ, sa papəʦəvuŋi a ʦaɣis a səma ʦua kakəɖian; sa viʎviʎi a ʦaɣis.
S.TRANSL: Vuluvulung went round the woods, and brought the ends of the cord together to where the child was, and pulled on the cord.

S.FORM: qau mapengeḍuq a kasiw a mapuɫat.
S.PHON: qau mapəŋəɖuq a kasiw a mapuʎat.
S.FORM: qau mapengedruq a kasiw a mapuljat.
S.PHON: qau mapəŋəɖuq a kasiw a mapuʎat.
S.TRANSL: And all the trees were snapped off.

S.FORM: vaik a sema tapaw.
S.PHON: vaik a səma tapaw.
S.FORM: vaik a sema tapaw.
S.PHON: vaik a səma tapaw.
S.TRANSL: They went back to the house.

S.FORM: tsuatsuay anga pagaɫu matsay a kasiw.
S.PHON: ʦuaʦuaj aŋa pagaʎu maʦaj a kasiw.
S.FORM: cuacuay anga pagalju macay a kasiw.
S.PHON: ʦuaʦuaj aŋa pagaʎu maʦaj a kasiw.
S.TRANSL: After quite a long time the trees died;

S.FORM: ɫamain, sa tjuguti tua lavu nua vaqu.
S.PHON: ʎamain, sa cuguti tua ɭavu nua vaqu.
S.FORM: ljamain, sa tjuguti tua lavu nua vaqu.
S.PHON: ʎamain, sa ʦuguti tua ɣavu nua vaqu.
S.TRANSL: she burnt them off, and sowed millet chaff.

S.FORM: tsuatsuay anga pagaɫu, "sa-u, kiqenetji a tja tjinugut," ayain azua aɫak ni sa Vuluvulung.
S.PHON: ʦuaʦuaj aŋa pagaʎu, "sa-u, kiqənəci a ca cinugut," ajain azua aʎak ni sa vuɭuvuɭuŋ.
S.FORM: cuacuay anga pagalju, "sa-u, kiqenetji a tja tjinugut," ayain azua aljak ni sa Vuluvulung.
S.PHON: ʦuaʦuaj aŋa pagaʎu, "sa-u, kiqənəʦi a ʦa ʦinugut," ajain azua aʎak ni sa vuɣuvuɣuŋ.
S.TRANSL: After quite a long time Vuluvulung said to the child: “Go and look at what we sowed.”

S.FORM: vaik azua aɫak a kiqenetj.
S.PHON: vaik azua aʎak a kiqənəc.
S.FORM: vaik azua aljak a kiqenetj.
S.PHON: vaik azua aʎak a kiqənəʦ.
S.TRANSL: The child went to look,

S.FORM: mangtjez a aɫak a nakiqenetj.
S.PHON: maŋcəz a aʎak a nakiqənəc.
S.FORM: mangtjez a aljak a nakiqenetj.
S.PHON: maŋʦəz a aʎak a nakiqənəʦ.
S.TRANSL: and came back after looking.

S.FORM: "neka nu anema, vuvu," aya azua kakeḍian.
S.PHON: "nəka nu anəma, vuvu," aja azua kakəɖian.
S.FORM: "neka nu anema, vuvu," aya azua kakedrian.
S.PHON: "nəka nu anəma, vuvu," aja azua kakəɖian.
S.TRANSL: “There’s nothing, granny,” said the child.

S.FORM: "pasaladji; nanguaq nu tsuay anga," aya ti sa Vuluvulung.
S.PHON: "pasaɭaɉi; naŋuaq nu ʦuaj aŋa," aja ti sa vuɭuvuɭuŋ.
S.FORM: "pasaladji; nanguaq nu cuay anga," aya ti sa Vuluvulung.
S.PHON: "pasaɣaɉi; naŋuaq nu ʦuaj aŋa," aja ti sa vuɣuvuɣuŋ.
S.TRANSL: “Come along; it will be all right in time,” said Vuluvulung.

S.FORM: tsuatsuay anga a uta pagaɫu, "sa-u, kiqenetji anan," ayain ni sa Vuluvulung.
S.PHON: ʦuaʦuaj aŋa a uta pagaʎu, "sa-u, kiqənəci anan," ajain ni sa vuɭuvuɭuŋ.
S.FORM: cuacuay anga a uta pagalju, "sa-u, kiqenetji anan," ayain ni sa Vuluvulung.
S.PHON: ʦuaʦuaj aŋa a uta pagaʎu, "sa-u, kiqənəʦi anan," ajain ni sa vuɣuvuɣuŋ.
S.TRANSL: After quite a time more the child was told by Vuluvulung: “Go and have another look.”

S.FORM: vaik a kakeḍian.
S.PHON: vaik a kakəɖian.
S.FORM: vaik a kakedrian.
S.PHON: vaik a kakəɖian.
S.TRANSL: The child went,

S.FORM: qau mangtjez a uta.
S.PHON: qau maŋcəz a uta.
S.FORM: qau mangtjez a uta.
S.PHON: qau maŋʦəz a uta.
S.TRANSL: and came back again.

S.FORM: "izuanga, vuvu, a galavagavagaw a keḍikeḍian, vuvu; qadjaw ki anema," aya azua kakeḍian.
S.PHON: "izuaŋa, vuvu, a gaɭavagavagaw a kəɖikəɖian, vuvu; qaɉaw ki anəma," aja azua kakəɖian.
S.FORM: "izuanga, vuvu, a galavagavagaw a kedrikedrian, vuvu; qadjaw ki anema," aya azua kakedrian.
S.PHON: "izuaŋa, vuvu, a gaɣavagavagaw a kəɖikəɖian, vuvu; qaɉaw ki anəma," aja azua kakəɖian.
S.TRANSL: “Granny, now there are some small things wriggling about, granny. I don’t know what they are,” said the child.

S.FORM: "pasaladji; nanguaq nu tsuay anga."
S.PHON: "pasaɭaɉi; naŋuaq nu ʦuaj aŋa."
S.FORM: "pasaladji; nanguaq nu cuay anga."
S.PHON: "pasaɣaɉi; naŋuaq nu ʦuaj aŋa."
S.TRANSL: “Come along; it will be all right in time.”

S.FORM: tsuatsuay pagaɫu a uta, "sa-u, kiqenetji anan," ayain ni sa Vuluvulung.
S.PHON: ʦuaʦuaj pagaʎu a uta, "sa-u, kiqənəci anan," ajain ni sa vuɭuvuɭuŋ.
S.FORM: cuacuay pagalju a uta, "sa-u, kiqenetji anan," ayain ni sa Vuluvulung.
S.PHON: ʦuaʦuaj pagaʎu a uta, "sa-u, kiqənəʦi anan," ajain ni sa vuɣuvuɣuŋ.
S.TRANSL: After quite a time more the child was told by Vuluvulung: “Go and have another look.”

S.FORM: vaik a kakeḍian a kiqenetj; mangtjez.
S.PHON: vaik a kakəɖian a kiqənəc; maŋcəz.
S.FORM: vaik a kakedrian a kiqenetj; mangtjez.
S.PHON: vaik a kakəɖian a kiqənəʦ; maŋʦəz.
S.TRANSL: The child went to look, and came back.

S.FORM: "izuanga, vuvu, a kuḍakuḍalan a pagaɫu; qadjaw ki anema," aya azua kakeḍian.
S.PHON: "izuaŋa, vuvu, a kuɖakuɖaɭan a pagaʎu; qaɉaw ki anəma," aja azua kakəɖian.
S.FORM: "izuanga, vuvu, a kudrakudralan a pagalju; qadjaw ki anema," aya azua kakedrian.
S.PHON: "izuaŋa, vuvu, a kuɖakuɖaɣan a pagaʎu; qaɉaw ki anəma," aja azua kakəɖian.
S.TRANSL: “Now there are some quite big things, granny. I don’t know what they are,” said the child.

S.FORM: "pasaladji anan," aya ti sa Vuluvulung.
S.PHON: "pasaɭaɉi anan," aja ti sa vuɭuvuɭuŋ.
S.FORM: "pasaladji anan," aya ti sa Vuluvulung.
S.PHON: "pasaɣaɉi anan," aja ti sa vuɣuvuɣuŋ.
S.TRANSL: “Come along still (ie. let’s leave them),” said Vuluvulung.

S.FORM: tsuatsuay anga pagaɫu a uta, vaiken ni sa Vuluvulung katua aɫak a kiqenetj.
S.PHON: ʦuaʦuaj aŋa pagaʎu a uta, vaikən ni sa vuɭuvuɭuŋ katua aʎak a kiqənəc.
S.FORM: cuacuay anga pagalju a uta, vaiken ni sa Vuluvulung katua aljak a kiqenetj.
S.PHON: ʦuaʦuaj aŋa pagaʎu a uta, vaikən ni sa vuɣuvuɣuŋ katua aʎak a kiqənəʦ.
S.TRANSL: After quite a time more Vuluvulung and the child went to have a look at them.

S.FORM: manu a uta kuḍalan anga, a marka venan, a vavuy, a marka sizi, marka takets, aya.
S.PHON: manu a uta kuɖaɭan aŋa, a marka vənan, a vavuj, a marka sizi, marka takəʦ, aja.
S.FORM: manu a uta kudralan anga, a marka venan, a vavuy, a marka sizi, marka takec, aya.
S.PHON: manu a uta kuɖaɣan aŋa, a maRka vənan, a vavuj, a maRka sizi, maRka takəʦ, aja.
S.TRANSL: And now they were big things-deer, wild pig, goats and muntjacs, they say.

S.FORM: qau mavan anga nu sika izua nua venan nua vavuy nua sizi nua takets tutsu, aya tjautsikel a masi tsuay.
S.PHON: qau mavan aŋa nu sika izua nua vənan nua vavuj nua sizi nua takəʦ tuʦu, aja cauʦikəɭ a masi ʦuaj.
S.FORM: qau mavan anga nu sika izua nua venan nua vavuy nua sizi nua takec tucu, aya tjaucikel a masi cuay.
S.PHON: qau mavan aŋa nu sika izua nua vənan nua vavuj nua sizi nua takəʦ tuʦu, aja ʦauʦikəɣ a masi ʦuaj.
S.TRANSL: And that’s why there are deer, wild pigs, goats and muntjacs now, according to the old story.
